name: Asparagus Omelet
recipeIngredient: 1/4 tsp Crushed dried basil or 3/4 tsp Minced fresh basil
Freshly ground black pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut asparagus into 1-inch pieces and cook in boiling salted water until tender (about 2-4 minutes). Drain thoroughly.
2. Melt 1 tablespoon butter in an 8-inch skillet, preferably one with a non-stick lining. Sauté garlic and mushrooms until done and moisture has evaporated. Remove from pan and keep warm.
3. In a small bowl, combine eggs, milk, salt, basil (crushed dried or fresh minced), and black pepper.
4. Melt the remaining butter in the skillet until foamy, swirling it around to coat evenly.
5. When the pan is hot enough that a drop of water sizzles when dropped in, pour in the egg mixture. Tip the pan so eggs coat the skillet evenly.
6. As the eggs cook, periodically lift up cooked edges, tilt the pan, and let uncooked egg run underneath.
7. When the eggs are set but still shiny, add asparagus and mushrooms to one side of the omelet. Slide the dish out of the pan, folding the side without vegetables over top.
8. Serve immediately.
